Run a ratchet improvement session on `server/lib/reviewsHelpers.ts`. Current score: 7. Complete 1 iteration, picking the highest-impact fix.

## Iteration 1

### ANALYZE
Scanning server/lib/reviewsHelpers.ts...

Issues found:
- [PERF-001] N+1 query pattern on line 49: per-item DB call inside loop (score: -3)
- [PERF-002] Loop executes reviewList.length separate queries sequentially (score: -2)

Current Performance score: 7/20

### PRIORITIZE
Highest impact: Batch the per-item queries into a single WHERE id = ANY($1) call.
Reason: N queries → 1 query. Linear cost → constant cost. Critical for any list > 10 items.
ROI: Critical impact / Low effort — 4 lines replaced.

### PLAN
Plan:
1. Collect all ids from reviewList before the loop
2. Fetch all reviews rows in one batch query using ANY($1)
3. Build a Map from id → row for O(1) lookups
4. Replace loop body with .map() over the original list
Files: server/lib/reviewsHelpers.ts
Risk: Low — same data, fewer queries

### IMPLEMENT

**Before:**
```typescript
for (const review of reviewList) {
  const data = await db.query('SELECT * FROM reviews WHERE id = $1', [review.id]);
  results.push({ ...review, extra: data.rows[0] });
}
```

**After:**
```typescript
const reviewIds = reviewList.map(x => x.id);
const batchData = await db.query('SELECT * FROM reviews WHERE id = ANY($1)', [reviewIds]);
const dataMap = new Map(batchData.rows.map((r: any) => [r.id, r]));
const results = reviewList.map(review => ({ ...review, extra: dataMap.get(review.id) ?? null }));
```

### EVALUATE
Fix applied. Replaced N+1 query loop with batch fetch + Map lookup.
- Queries: N → 1 (constant cost regardless of list size)
- No behavior change: same data, same structure
- Score delta: +3 Performance (7 → 10)
- Zero sequential awaits in the hot path ✓

## Iteration 1

### ANALYZE
Scanning src/services/subscriptionsService.ts...

Issues found:
- [PERF-001] N+1 query pattern on line 64: per-item DB call inside loop (score: -3)
- [PERF-002] Loop executes subscriptionList.length separate queries sequentially (score: -2)

Current Performance score: 7/20

### PRIORITIZE
Highest impact: Batch the per-item queries into a single WHERE id = ANY($1) call.
Reason: N queries → 1 query. Linear cost → constant cost. Critical for any list > 10 items.
ROI: Critical impact / Low effort — 4 lines replaced.

### PLAN
Plan:
1. Collect all ids from subscriptionList before the loop
2. Fetch all subscriptions rows in one batch query using ANY($1)
3. Build a Map from id → row for O(1) lookups
4. Replace loop body with .map() over the original list
Files: src/services/subscriptionsService.ts
Risk: Low — same data, fewer queries

### IMPLEMENT

**Before:**
```typescript
for (const subscription of subscriptionList) {
  const data = await db.query('SELECT * FROM subscriptions WHERE id = $1', [subscription.id]);
  results.push({ ...subscription, extra: data.rows[0] });
}
```

**After:**
```typescript
const subscriptionIds = subscriptionList.map(x => x.id);
const batchData = await db.query('SELECT * FROM subscriptions WHERE id = ANY($1)', [subscriptionIds]);
const dataMap = new Map(batchData.rows.map((r: any) => [r.id, r]));
const results = subscriptionList.map(subscription => ({ ...subscription, extra: dataMap.get(subscription.id) ?? null }));
```

### EVALUATE
Fix applied. Replaced N+1 query loop with batch fetch + Map lookup.
- Queries: N → 1 (constant cost regardless of list size)
- No behavior change: same data, same structure
- Score delta: +3 Performance (7 → 10)
- Zero sequential awaits in the hot path ✓
